content_review: NOT_APPLICABLE — no SSOT content files (biomarkers.yaml, lifestyle_registry.yaml, questionnaire.json) in scope. Changes are confined to: PDF parse prompt, LLM parser model and response, and frontend upload-review seam.
behaviour_review: COMPLETE — MIXED correct. Sprint changes PDF extraction behaviour (what the LLM returns in the reference string), parse response contract (new or enriched fields), and upload-review attention classification (new one-sided state distinct from partial). These changes affect user-input interpretation upstream of analysis, even though scoring policy and analytics pipeline are not touched. Determinism: LLM parse output is inherently non-deterministic (pre-existing condition). rangeAttentionLevel() and buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow() are deterministic given the same parse output — no change to this guarantee. referenceRangeToPayload() must remain unchanged (payload contract). Regression: edit flow and analysis payload handoff must survive — EditDialog.tsx and the payload shape must not break.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:3 — branch: feature/pdf-parse-one-sided-ranges-and-rich-reference-text

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:5 — HIGH. Stage 1C (lines 92-105): sprint changes governed parse extraction truth and review classification behaviour for user-supplied lab data before analysis submission. No downgrade permitted.
automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:6 — TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:7 — MIXED: PDF parse extraction (BEHAVIOUR) + parse response contract (BEHAVIOUR) + frontend attention classification (BEHAVIOUR) + prompt/schema content update (CONTENT). MIXED governs using BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4.

backend/core/llm/prompts/parsing_prompt_pdf.txt:46 — 'Skip qualitative results, ranges, or non-numeric data'. This instruction actively prevents the LLM from preserving multi-line or contextual reference text (sex-specific, pregnancy, life-stage blocks). It is the primary upstream cause of contextual reference text being lost during PDF extraction. Must be removed or replaced. Also: schema at lines 4-18 has no raw_reference_text or comparator field — LLM cannot return structured contextual blocks or strict inequality semantics. Lines 52-57 describe reference range parsing but do not instruct the LLM to preserve strict vs weak inequality symbols (< vs ≤, > vs ≥) in the reference string.

changes.check: 2A — backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py exists; LLMParser path confirmed for PDF
changes.citation: backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:48-50 — 'application/pdf' maps to 'parsing_prompt_pdf.txt'. Lines 278-281: PDF files sent as raw multimodal input (input_files) directly to Gemini — prompt template is used as-is, no text conversion. Lines 223-243: _parse_gemini_response() calls _parse_reference_range(biomarker_data.get('reference', '')) for each biomarker. This confirms parsing_prompt_pdf.txt is the sole authority prompt for the live PDF parse path.

changes.check: 2A — llm_parser.py ParsedBiomarker model and response dict confirmed; no raw_reference_text field
changes.citation: backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:21-31 — ParsedBiomarker fields: id: str, name: str, value: float, unit: str, reference: str, confidence: float, ref_low: Optional[float], ref_high: Optional[float], status: Optional[str]. No raw_reference_text or comparator field. Lines 333-343: response dict emits { id, name, value, unit, 'referenceRange': biomarker.reference (raw string), confidence, ref_low, ref_high, healthStatus }. No 'reference' key in the dict — raw string goes under 'referenceRange'.

backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:161-188 — patterns at lines 172-176 handle < (returns None, float), > (returns float, None), ≤ (returns None, float), ≥ (returns float, None). Logic at lines 180-186 correctly maps these. One-sided ref_low/ref_high correctly extracted from the reference string when present. However: comparator semantics (< vs ≤, > vs ≥) are lost — only the numeric bound is returned. The reference string must therefore preserve the exact symbol for the frontend display layer to recover it via _comparatorBeforeBound (uploadReferenceRange.ts:152-191).

backend/app/routes/upload.py:333-343 — biomarker dict keys confirmed: id, name, value, unit, referenceRange (= biomarker.reference raw string), confidence, ref_low, ref_high, healthStatus. Lines 128-145: SSOT enrichment adds 'ssot' block but does not touch range fields. No structural change needed in upload.py unless a new raw_reference_text field is added to ParsedBiomarker — in that case line 333 must emit it under a key readable by buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow (which reads b.reference at uploadReferenceRange.ts:34).

frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:30-78 — buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow: reads b.reference (string, line 34) and b.referenceRange (string, line 37) into referenceText. Reads b.ref_low (line 41) and b.ref_high (line 42) for numeric bounds. Frontend correctly receives one-sided bounds when backend sends ref_low or ref_high. _comparatorBeforeBound (lines 152-191) already recovers < vs > from referenceText. This pipeline is correct and must be preserved.

changes.check: 2A — rangeAttentionLevel confirmed: one-sided currently returns 'partial', not 'missing'
changes.citation: frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:83-95 — rangeAttentionLevel: (hasMin && hasMax) → 'none'; (hasMin || hasMax || hasText) → 'partial'; neither → 'missing'. A one-sided threshold (hasMin XOR hasMax) correctly returns 'partial', not 'missing'. But ParsedTable.tsx:56-63 renders 'partial' as badge label 'Range incomplete' with AlertTriangle — this label is misleading for valid one-sided thresholds. RangeAttention type (line 6) is 'none' | 'partial' | 'missing' — no distinct 'one-sided' state exists. Sprint must add 'one-sided' as a new distinct state and update both rangeAttentionLevel and ParsedTable.

frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx:33-64 — rangeBadge(): att === 'missing' → 'Range needed' badge (amber/secondary). att === 'partial' → 'Range incomplete' badge (amber/outline). No 'one-sided' branch. After rangeAttentionLevel is updated to emit 'one-sided', ParsedTable must add a new branch for 'one-sided' with a distinct neutral label (e.g. 'One-sided range') rather than the alarming 'Range incomplete'. Existing 'partial' branch can remain for text-only partial cases.

frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx:79-433 — EditDialog already supports referenceText display with copy button and unlock-to-edit flow (lines 347-398). Min/max/unit edit fields intact (lines 309-344). parseBiomarkerValueForReview validates value field (line 123). Edit flow is already correct from BE-W1-PR2 — no changes required in this sprint. Payload handoff via referenceRangeToPayload() (uploadReferenceRange.ts:100-113) is unaffected.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/types/parsed.ts exists; type coverage confirmed sufficient
changes.citation: frontend/app/types/parsed.ts:22-27 — ParsedBiomarker already has referenceRange?: { min?: number; max?: number; unit: string } and referenceText?: string. RangeAttention type change is in uploadReferenceRange.ts not parsed.ts — no type changes needed in parsed.ts unless a new comparator field is added to ParsedBiomarker. Not required: _comparatorBeforeBound already reads the symbol from referenceText at runtime.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx alert banner; one-sided attention check confirmed
changes.citation: frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx:354-370 — amber alert triggered when any marker has rangeAttentionLevel !== 'none'. After 'one-sided' is added as a new state, one-sided thresholds will still trigger this alert unless the condition is updated to exclude 'one-sided'. Prompt §1 (lines 113-119): valid one-sided thresholds must not be treated as incomplete. Alert banner logic should be updated to exclude 'one-sided' from its trigger condition — it is not an attention-required case.

changes.check: 2A — No duplicate authority source for reference ranges
changes.citation: Single authority chain confirmed: lab PDF → parsing_prompt_pdf.txt → LLMParser → upload.py → buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow → review/edit → referenceRangeToPayload → analysis payload. No SSOT range registry accessed in this path. STOP condition 3 not triggered.

changes.check: 2A — Forbidden files confirmed not in scope

NOT touched: backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, backend/scripts/run_work_package.py, backend/scripts/golden_gate_local.py, backend/scripts/update_cursor_status.py.

changes.check: 2B — Behavioural scope defined; all Intelligence Core confirmed not touched
changes.citation: Prompt non-goals (lines 214-222): no scoring-policy changes, no fallback-range behaviour, no broad parser redesign, no results-page/narrative work, no questionnaire work, no second authority source. Sprint scope: parsing_prompt_pdf.txt (extraction prompt), llm_parser.py (model + response), uploadReferenceRange.ts (RangeAttention type + rangeAttentionLevel + formatReferenceRangeDisplay), ParsedTable.tsx (badge for one-sided), page.tsx (alert condition). Intelligence Core (pipeline, analytics, InsightGraph) not touched.

changes.check: 2B — Regression targets confirmed and testable within scope
changes.citation: Prompt §Regression Targets (lines 268-293): (1) parser captures comparator semantics — testable via parsing_prompt_pdf.txt change + unit test on _parse_reference_range with inequality input; (2) one-sided thresholds not mislabeled — testable via updated rangeAttentionLevel returning 'one-sided'; (3) rich reference text visible — testable via ParsedTable rendering referenceText; (4) truly missing data still flagged — rangeAttentionLevel 'missing' path unchanged; (5) edit flow intact — EditDialog.tsx unchanged; (6) payload serialization — referenceRangeToPayload() unchanged.

STOP1: no scoring policy change required. STOP2: prompt/model update is bounded — not an open-ended parser rewrite. STOP3: no second authority source — rich reference text is from the lab document itself. STOP4: one-sided vs incomplete IS distinguishable via hasMin XOR hasMax in rangeAttentionLevel — clean distinction. STOP5: changes bounded to parse extraction prompt + parser model + review classification — no broad redesign. STOP6: reference text preserved as structured field in existing response contract — not free-form unparseable output.

Risk 1: LLM parse output non-determinism (pre-existing) — same PDF may return different reference strings across calls. Sprint does not introduce or worsen this. Risk 2: If parsing_prompt_pdf.txt instructs LLM to preserve multi-line text in the reference string, longer/richer text may occasionally exceed LLM context or truncate. Cursor must keep the field bounded and instruct the LLM on a maximum length where appropriate. Risk 3: rangeAttentionLevel adding 'one-sided' state — caller sites in page.tsx and ParsedTable.tsx must be updated together; partial updates would leave TypeScript type mismatch. Cursor must update all three files atomically.
